title: How to Automate Your Trading Journal in Notion (Step by Step)
subtitle: A 30-minute setup that ends manual journaling forever.
excerpt: Build an automated Notion trading journal that pulls in fills from your broker, tags trades by setup and gives you weekly performance reviews.

blocks.text: 30 minutes of setup with Make.com + Notion API → every fill auto-logs to a journal page. Bonus: weekly AI summary every Sunday.
Manual trade journaling is the #1 thing serious traders quit. Solution: don't do it manually. Here's the exact stack to make your journal self-update.

items: Make.com is the glue between your broker and Notion (free tier is enough).
One template database in Notion holds every trade with rich properties.
ChatGPT writes your weekly review automatically every Sunday at 6 PM.

steps.title: Create the Notion database
steps.body: Properties: ticker, side, entry, exit, P&L, R, setup tag, screenshot, notes, date.

steps.title: Get a Notion API token
steps.body: Go to notion.so/my-integrations → New integration → copy the token. Share your database with the integration.

steps.title: Connect your broker to Make.com
steps.body: If your broker has a webhook (TradingView alerts, IBKR, Alpaca), point it at a Make.com webhook trigger.

steps.title: Create the Make scenario
steps.body: Webhook → Notion 'Create database item' → done. Map each field.

steps.title: Add the weekly summary
steps.body: Schedule a Sunday 6 PM Make scenario: Notion 'List items' (last 7 days) → ChatGPT 'Summarize trades' → Notion 'Append to journal'.

blocks.title: Free for most retail traders
blocks.text: Make.com free tier handles 1,000 ops/month, easily enough for active swing traders.

items: Setup tag (so you can analyze edge per setup)

links.label: Best AI tools for swing traders

faqs.q: Do I need to know how to code?
faqs.a: No. Make.com is fully visual. The hardest step is reading Notion's API page, about 10 minutes.

faqs.q: Will this work with my broker?
faqs.a: If your broker supports webhooks or API access (or you route via TradingView alerts), yes.
$0 for most retail traders. Notion free + Make.com free + ChatGPT free is enough to start.

seo.metaTitle: Automate Trading Journal in Notion (Step-by-Step Tutorial)
seo.metaDescription: Build a self-updating trading journal in Notion using Make.com. 30 minutes of setup, zero manual entry from then on.
